$comment: Partner MCP servers registered as tools in the SilentOps audit workflow (Google Cloud Agent Builder). Track-qualifying integration per the Rapid Agent Hackathon. Fill the env-referenced secrets at deploy time; do NOT commit real credentials.

phoenix.description: Arize Phoenix MCP server (@arizeai/phoenix-mcp) — the Arize-track-qualifying server. Lets the agent introspect its own operational data at runtime: traces, spans, sessions, datasets, experiments, prompts. Used at the INGEST boundary (pull traces) and to write regression eval cases back as dataset examples.
phoenix.notes: Verified against @arizeai/phoenix-mcp tool surface (2026-06). Traces must be sent to Phoenix Cloud (app.phoenix.arize.com) for the MCP to introspect them. Raw traces/spans returned here are passed to the Normalizer and redacted/masked before any reasoning — the Gemini auditor never sees raw values.

mongodb.description: MongoDB MCP server (mongodb-mcp-server) — audit memory: store findings/clusters, search failure patterns, persist regression eval cases. Used at the MEMORY boundary on already-privacy-safe records.
mongodb.notes: Confirm exact tool names against the deployed mongodb-mcp-server version. Only privacy-safe documents (entity types, redacted snippets, hashes) are written — never raw sensitive values.
